mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters. It simplifies the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. This operator is ideal for users looking to automate RabbitMQ deployments in Kubernetes environments.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm setup, allowing you to install the operator easily.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://rabbitmq.github.io/cluster-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the RabbitMQ repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which will manage RabbitMQ clusters in your Kubernetes environment.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq --create-namespace

```

> Installs the operator in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined YAML configuration file.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq

```

> Lists all pods in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.
> Lists all RabbitMQ clusters in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if the RabbitMQ clusters are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq

```

> Uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Deletes the 'rabbitmq' namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your RabbitMQ data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version while ensuring the existing clusters remain functional.

```bash

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> Backs up the current RabbitMQ cluster configurations.
> Upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Verifies that the RabbitMQ pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources in the Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your Kubernetes cluster has enough resources (minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: unable to recognize '"rabbitmq.com/v1beta1'"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Custom Resource Definition (CRD) for RabbitMQ is not installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator to ensure CRDs are applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RabbitMQ configuration is incorrect or if there are issues with the underlying storage.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the RabbitMQ pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RabbitMQ cluster not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the RabbitMQ pods are not fully initialized.

**Fix:**
Wait for a few minutes and check the status again using 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, improved logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods running with a status of 'Running' and the RabbitMQ cluster listed as ready when executing 'kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster with the necessary tools installed.
